_curation.note: Hand-curated. `id` is the stable Claude Code request value (model_selection = alias). Facts seeded from the pinned @earendil-works/pi-ai `anthropic` block; label/description/ratings written by a human from the current lineup. The live harness can add or remove context-hinted variants as account access changes, so the catalog uses the stable bare Fable id that the runner can widen to a `[1m]` option when available. The current Anthropic frontier is Fable 5, above Opus, so descriptions and ratings place Opus one tier below it. Ratings: 1-5, higher is better; `cost` is cost-efficiency (5 = cheapest).
_curation.sources: @earendil-works/pi-ai@0.80.6 anthropic block; Anthropic model + pricing pages

models.label: Default
models.description: The harness's recommended default model. Resolves to whatever the Claude Code build selects.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 5
models.label: Sonnet
models.description: Balanced speed and capability for everyday agentic work, cheaper than Opus.

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.label: Haiku
models.description: The fastest, cheapest tier. Use for high-volume, latency-sensitive tasks.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.label: Opus (1M context)
models.description: Strong reasoning below Fable, at a lower price, with the 1M-token context window.

models.name: Claude Fable 5
models.label: Fable
models.description: The current Anthropic frontier: the strongest reasoning tier, above Opus.
